title: Corymbia henryi
taxonomy: Corymbia henryi is classified within the family Myrtaceae, which includes a wide range of flowering plants commonly known as the myrtle family. This family contains over 3,000 species, many of which are native to Australia and surrounding regions. Corymbia henryi is further classified within the genus Corymbia, a group of eucalyptus trees native to Australia. The genus Corymbia is divided into several species, and Corymbia henryi is one of the many species found within this group. The classification of Corymbia henryi highlights its unique characteristics and relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Corymbia henryi, also known as the Queensland peppermint gum, is a species of flowering plant in the myrtle family, Myrtaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to aid in digestion and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, as well as to reduce fever and relieve headaches, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Corymbia henryi contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to take advantage of its health benefits.
